chapter: Methods of Separation in Everyday Life

questions.question_html: What purpose does handpicking serve in the process of separation?
Which of the following substances are commonly separated using the churning method?
Which factor is usually essential for the filtration?
Salt can be separated from salt solution by keeping it under the Sun.
Handpicking should be used only when the quantity of one component is less.
A mixture of puffed rice and rice grains can be separated by threshing.

options.explanation: Corrected Statement: A mixture of puffed rice and rice grains can be separated by winnowing.

questions.question_html: A mixture of mustard oil and lemon water can be separated by decantation.
Sieving is used to separate a mixture of rice flour and water.

options.explanation: Corrected Statement: Sieving is used to separate a mixture of different sized solid particles, not for separating solid from liquid.

questions.question_html:
Match the mixtures in Column I with their method of separation in Column II.

Column I Column II
(i) Gram flour mixed with black gram (a) Handpicking
(ii) Chalk powder mixed with water (b) Magnetic separation
(iii) Corn mixed with potatoes (c) Decantation
(iv) Iron powder mixed with sawdust (d) Sieving
 (v) Oil mixed with water (e) Filtration
questions.solution_html:
Column I Column II
(i) Gram flour mixed with black gram (a) Sieving
(ii) Chalk powder mixed with water (b) Filtration
(iii) Corn mixed with potatoes (c) Handpicking
(iv) Iron powder mixed with sawdust (d) Magnetic separation
 (v) Oil mixed with water (e) Decantation

questions.question_html: In what situations would you use decantation instead of filtration to separate solids from liquids?
questions.solution_html: Decantation is used when the solid particles are heavy and settle down at the bottom of the container, allowing the clear liquid to be poured off without disturbing the solid sediment. For example, separating sand from water.

questions.question_html: Can you relate the presence of nasal hair to any separation process?
questions.solution_html: Nasal hair acts like a natural filter, trapping dust, pollen, and other particles from the air we breathe, similar to how a filtration process works to remove solid impurities from a liquid.

questions.question_html: During the COVID-19 pandemic, all of us wore masks. Generally, what material are they made of? What is the role of these masks?
questions.solution_html: Masks are generally made of multiple layers of fabric or materials such as cotton, polypropylene, or polyester. Their role is to filter out harmful particles, including viruses, from the air we breathe, similar to how filtration processes work to remove impurities from liquids.

questions.question_html: A mixture containing potatoes, salt and sawdust has been given to you. Outline a stepwise procedure for separating each component from this mixture.
questions.solution_html:
1. Step 1: Handpick the potatoes out of the mixture.
  2. Step 2: Add water to the remaining mixture to dissolve the salt.
  3. Step 3: Filter the mixture to separate the sawdust from the salt solution.
  4. Step 4: Evaporate the water from the salt solution to obtain the salt.

questions.question_html:
Read the following story titled Intelligent Leela and tick the most appropriate options. Provide a suitable title of your choice for the paragraph.
Leela was working in the farm with her father when she realised that they left their drinking water at home. Before her father felt thirsty/hungry, she went to the nearby pond to fetch some water/grains. After obtaining some water in the container, she noticed that the water was muddy and fit/unfit for drinking. To purify the water, she kept it for some time and then she filtered/churned the muddy water using a piece of paper/muslin cloth. Leela, then, cooled/boiled the water for about 10 minutes in a covered pan. After cooling/boiling, she filtered/churned it again and made it fit/unfit for drinking. She served this water to her father while having food, who blessed her and appreciated her efforts.
